title: Tetrapterys benthamii
taxonomy: Tetrapterys benthamii belongs to the family Malpighiaceae. This plant is a part of the order Malpighiales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is further categorized under the subclass Rosidae and the division Magnoliophyta. Within the kingdom Plantae, Tetrapterys benthamii holds a specific place among the flowering plants. The classification of Tetrapterys benthamii is based on its morphological and genetic characteristics.

intro: Tetrapterys benthamii, also known as "Mololo" in some regions, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Tetrapterys benthamii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
